description: Wire all missing create/submit flows (Polls, ScheduledMsgs, Q&A, Events, Wiki, Bots, Shop), add missing backend endpoints (mutuals, status, widgets, badges), add loading skeletons + 404 + error boundaries, and align key screens to gratonite.pen mockups.

userStories.title: PollsPage — Create Poll modal
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a poll from PollsPage so that I can gather votes from my community.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Create Poll' button opens a modal
Modal form has: question text input (max 300 chars), at least 2 answer option inputs (up to 10, add/remove buttons), allow multiselect toggle, optional duration select (1h / 24h / 7d / No expiry)
On submit: calls api.polls.create(channelId, { question, answers: [{text}], allowMultiselect, duration }) — channelId from useParams
On success: closes modal, invalidates polls query so list refreshes
Submit button disabled while loading; shows error message on failure
Also fix: PollsPage vote handler must call api.polls.vote(pollId, [answerId]) — verify it uses api.polls.vote not a raw fetch to /vote
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/web/src/pages/PollsPage.tsx. api.polls.create is at /channels/:channelId/polls. api.polls.vote is at POST /polls/:pollId/answers.

userStories.title: ScheduledMsgsPage — Schedule Message form
userStories.description: As a user, I want to schedule a message to be sent later so that I can plan announcements.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Schedule Message' button opens a modal
Modal form has: channel select (populated from api.guilds.getChannels(guildId)), message content textarea (max 4000 chars), date+time picker for scheduled time (must be in the future)
On submit: calls api.scheduledMessages.create(guildId, { channelId, content, scheduledFor: isoString })
On success: closes modal, invalidates scheduled messages query so list refreshes
Submit button disabled while loading; shows inline error on failure
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/web/src/pages/ScheduledMsgsPage.tsx. guildId from useParams. api.scheduledMessages.create at POST /guilds/:guildId/scheduled-messages.

userStories.title: QAPage — Ask Question modal
userStories.description: As a user, I want to submit a new question on the Q&A page so that others can answer it.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Ask Question' button opens a modal
Modal form has: question title input (required, max 300 chars), optional body/details textarea (max 2000 chars)
On submit: POST to /api/v1/qa/channels/${channelId}/questions with { title, body } — matching the existing raw fetch pattern in the page
On success: closes modal, refreshes questions list
Submit button disabled while loading; shows error on failure
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/web/src/pages/QAPage.tsx. channelId from useParams. Uses raw fetch (not api.ts) to match existing page pattern.

userStories.title: EventsPage — Create Event button + RSVP wiring
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create events and RSVP to them from the Events page.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Create Event' button navigates to /events/${guildId}/create (CreateEventPage already exists)
Each event card has an RSVP/Interested toggle button
Clicking RSVP (not yet interested): calls api.events.setInterested(guildId, eventId) — PUT /guilds/:guildId/scheduled-events/:eventId/interested
Clicking again (already interested): calls api.events.removeInterested(guildId, eventId) — DELETE same endpoint
Button shows correct state (interested vs not) based on event data; optimistic UI update on click
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/web/src/pages/EventsPage.tsx. api.events.setInterested and removeInterested exist in api.ts at lines 1140/1143.

userStories.title: WikiPage — Add page handler
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create new wiki pages so that I can document my community.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking '+Add page' in sidebar shows an inline title input or small modal
On submit: calls api.wiki.create(channelId, { title, content: '' })
New page appears in sidebar page list and becomes the active/selected page
Empty state shown for new page with 'Start writing...' prompt
Typecheck passes

userStories.notes: File: apps/web/src/pages/WikiPage.tsx line 264. api.wiki.create at POST /channels/:channelId/wiki.

userStories.title: WikiPage — Edit, History, and More handlers
userStories.description: As a user, I want to edit wiki pages, view revision history, and delete/rename pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Edit' button: toggles page body into a markdown textarea editor pre-filled with current content; 'Save' button calls api.wiki.update(pageId, { title, content }); 'Cancel' reverts to view mode

Clicking 'History' button: calls api.wiki.getRevisions(pageId), shows a drawer/panel listing revisions with timestamps; clicking a revision shows its content; 'Revert' button calls api.wiki.revert(pageId, revisionId)
Clicking 'More' button: opens a small dropdown menu with 'Rename' and 'Delete' options
Rename: shows an inline title input, on confirm calls api.wiki.update(pageId, { title })
Delete: shows confirmation dialog, on confirm calls api.wiki.delete(pageId), navigates to first remaining page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/web/src/pages/WikiPage.tsx lines 292-294. api.wiki.update, delete, getRevisions, revert all exist in api.ts lines 1087-1099.

userStories.title: BotsPage — New Application + Configure dialog
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to create and configure bot applications from the Bots page.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'New Application' opens a modal with: app name input (required), description textarea (optional)
On submit: POST to /api/v1/bots with { name, description } (raw fetch, matching existing page pattern)
On success: refreshes bot list
Each bot in 'My Apps' tab has a 'Configure' button that opens a config modal showing: bot name (editable), description (editable), bot token (masked, with copy button), webhook URL input
Config modal Save button: PATCH to /api/v1/bots/${botId} with changed fields
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/web/src/pages/BotsPage.tsx lines 335, 428-429. Uses raw fetch (not api.ts namespace) matching existing page pattern.

userStories.title: ShopPage — Buy More Gratonites button
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 'Buy More' button in the Shop balance banner to take me somewhere to get more currency.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Buy More' button navigates to /gratonite (GratoniteDashboard) where the economy/rewards hub lives
Button uses useNavigate() from react-router-dom — no full page reload
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/web/src/pages/ShopPage.tsx line 702. Simple navigate fix — no backend needed.

userStories.title: Backend — Mutual friends/servers endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a /relationships/mutuals/:userId endpoint so that FriendsPage and UserProfilePage can show real mutual friends and servers.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /relationships/mutuals/:userId endpoint in apps/api/src/modules/relationships/relationships.router.ts (or service)
Returns { mutualFriends: Array<{id, username, displayName, avatarHash}>, mutualServers: Array<{id, name, iconHash}> }
mutualFriends: users who have an active 'friend' relationship with BOTH the current user AND the target user
mutualServers: guild IDs where both the current user and target user are members (join guild_members table)
Endpoint already present in api.ts at /users/${userId}/mutuals (line 498) — register the actual route handler
FriendsPage already shows mutual friends section — it will render real data once backend returns it
UserProfilePage already calls api.users.getMutuals(userId) — will work once backend returns real data
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: api.ts line 496-498 already has the frontend call to /users/:userId/mutuals. Backend needs to implement the handler.

userStories.title: Backend — Custom status API + SettingsPage wiring
userStories.description: As a user, I want my custom status to save to the server so it persists across devices.
acceptanceCriteria: Check packages/db/src/schema/users.ts — if statusText and statusExpiresAt columns do not exist, add them (text nullable, timestamptz nullable) — additive migration only
Add PATCH /users/@me/status endpoint in users router — body: { text: string | null, expiresAt: string | null }
Endpoint updates the user's statusText and statusExpiresAt in the DB
Add api.users.updateCustomStatus to apps/web/src/lib/api.ts: apiFetch('/users/@me/status', { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
In SettingsPage.tsx handleSaveStatus(): after calling saveProfileEnhancementsPrefs, also call api.users.updateCustomStatus({ text: statusInput.trim(), expiresAt })
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — Profile widgets save + UserProfilePage real data
userStories.description: As a user, I want the profile widgets I set in Settings to actually save and appear on my profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Check packages/db/src/schema/users.ts — if a widgets/profileWidgets JSONB column does not exist, add it (jsonb nullable default null) — additive migration only
Include widgets in GET /users/:id profile response (return from users service getProfile)
Add PATCH /users/@me/widgets endpoint — body: { widgets: string[] } — persists to DB
Add api.users.updateWidgets to apps/web/src/lib/api.ts
Wire SettingsPage widgets save button (currently calls saveProfileEnhancementsPrefs only) to also call api.users.updateWidgets(widgets)
UserProfilePage already reads (profile as any)?.widgets ?? [] — will show real data once backend returns it
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — Profile badges computed from real user data
userStories.description: As a user, I want my profile to show accurate badges based on my account data.
acceptanceCriteria: In GET /users/:id profile response (users service), include a computed badges: string[] field
Badge logic: 'Crystalline' if tier === 'crystalline'; 'OG' if createdAt year <= 2024; 'Staff' if isAdmin is true for this user
No new DB columns needed — badges are derived at query time
UserProfilePage already computes badges from (profile as any).badges — update it to use profile.badges directly (remove the local badges derivation added in previous session)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Loading skeletons — FriendsPage
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see loading skeletons while the friends list loads instead of an empty screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Destructure isLoading from the relationships useQuery call
While isLoading is true: render 6-8 skeleton rows in the friends list — grey pulsing bars for avatar and name
Use the existing Skeleton component from apps/web/src/components/ui/ if it exists, otherwise create a simple inline pulse with CSS animation
Once loaded: renders real friend rows as before
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/web/src/pages/FriendsPage.tsx lines 51-54. Check apps/web/src/components/ui/Skeleton.tsx exists.

userStories.title: Loading skeletons — UserProfilePage

acceptanceCriteria: While mutuals is loading: show 2-3 skeleton chips in mutual friends and mutual servers sections instead of empty
While wallet is loading (isOwnProfile): show '— G' with a subtle pulse animation instead of '0 G'
While friends list is loading: show skeleton rows in left sidebar

userStories.title: Loading skeletons — SettingsPage
userStories.description: As a user, I want a loading indicator in Settings while my profile data is refreshing.
acceptanceCriteria: While profile state is null (API call in progress): show a subtle spinner or skeleton in the account section avatar/display name area
Auth store values (user.displayName, user.avatarHash) still pre-fill the form fields as fallback — just add a small 'Refreshing...' indicator near the avatar
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: 404 catch-all route
userStories.description: As a user, I want a proper 404 page instead of a blank screen when I navigate to an unknown URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/pages/NotFoundPage.tsx with on-brand design: charcoal background, large '404' in gold accent, short message ('Page not found'), and a 'Go Home' button using useNavigate to '/'
Export as named export NotFoundPage
Add lazy import in App.tsx
Add <Route path='*' element={<NotFoundPage />} /> as the last route inside the authenticated Routes block
Navigating to /app/nonexistent-path shows the 404 page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: React error boundaries
userStories.description: As a user, I want a friendly error screen instead of a white crash when a component throws.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx as a React class component implementing componentDidCatch and getDerivedStateFromError
Fallback UI: centered panel with 'Something went wrong' message, error details (in dev only), and a 'Reload' button that calls window.location.reload()
Wrap the authenticated <AppLayout> route in App.tsx with <ErrorBoundary>
Wrap each major lazy-loaded page group (guild routes, DM routes, settings, shop) in a separate <ErrorBoundary> so one broken page does not crash the app
Typecheck passes
